steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The sentence says "One way to ______ the importance of a scholar's research is to track how often other scholars refer to that research." Tracking citations produces a judgment about significance, so the blank must name the act of evaluating or judging importance.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the right one
steps.content:
Substitute each choice into the sentence and test whether it matches evaluating importance through citation counts:
  • Choice A — 'assess': to evaluate or judge the value of something — tracking citations is exactly a method for assessing importance, and Malmendier's high citation count demonstrates her work's significance. ✓
  • Choice B — 'utilize': to make use of — you could utilize research, but tracking how often it is cited is not a way to "utilize" its importance. ✗
  • Choice C — 'influence': to have an effect on something — tracking citations does not influence importance; it measures it. ✗
  • Choice D — 'undermine': to weaken or damage — directly contradicts the passage, which describes citation tracking as a way to recognize importance, not diminish it. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
